source.title: Protein-altering and regulatory genetic variants near GATA4 implicated in bicuspid aortic valve

initial_request.research_problem: Bicuspid aortic valve (BAV) is a heritable congenital heart defect with unknown genetic origins. Previous studies (linkage, candidate sequencing, small GWAS) failed to identify robust genetic associations.
initial_request.known_context: BAV is heritable but genetic origins remain elusive; only one previous GWAS (n=68) with no significant findings.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: BAV is heritable but genetic origins remain elusive; previous GWAS underpowered (n=68) with no significant findings.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Lack of robust genetic associations for BAV.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A larger GWAS enriched for protein-altering variants will identify genetic variants associated with BAV.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design a GWAS with 466 cases and 4,660 controls, replication in up to 1,326 cases and 8,103 controls, using Illumina Human CoreExome array.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Perform GWAS to discover associated variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: Despite the prevalence, importance and heritability of BAV, its genetic origins remain elusive.
Only one previous genome-wide association study (GWAS) for BAV has been published in a limited number of cases (n=68; ref. 14), which did not identify any genome-wide significant results.
here we perform an unbiased genome scan in a large study of BAV cases (n=466) and controls (n=4,660), with replication in additional samples of up to 1,326 cases and 8,103 controls.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: GWAS designed with 466 cases and 4,660 controls.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to identify genetic variants associated with BAV.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Identified low-frequency intergenic variant rs6601627 (MAF=4.1%, OR=1.93, P=3.0e-15) 151 kb from GATA4, and common missense variant p.Ser377Gly in GATA4 (rs3729856, MAF=14.5%, OR=1.31, P=3.2e-6).

evidence.quote_or_span: Ser377Gly (rs3729856) in GATA4, which encodes a cardiac-specific transcription factor that is 151 kilobases(kb) away from the first variant ( $P_{after-replication}=8.8\times10^{-8}$ ).
Ser377Gly in GATA4 (rs3729856) that was also associated with BAV ( $P_{discovery} = 3.
Ser377Gly variant reached near genome-wide significance after including in silico replication data (OR = 1.31, $P = 8.
here we perform an unbiased genome scan in a large study of BAV cases (n=466) and controls (n=4,660), with replication in additional samples of up to 1,326 cases and 8,103 controls.
Ser377Gly) was associated with an increased risk of ToF, whereas the low-frequency noncoding variant (rs6601627) was not associated.
among the noncoding variant rs6601627 and its 115 correlated variants, 108 variants have MAF<0.01 and 73 are monomorphic in East Asians

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Discovery GWAS identified rs6601627 and rs3729856.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to confirm associations in independent samples.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Associations will replicate in additional cohorts.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Replicate in multiple cohorts (FCVC, MHI, Partners, UT, ASAP/ARTIST/POLCA, BioMe).
evolution_trajectory.observation: Replication confirmed association of rs6601627 and rs3729856; rs3729856 reached near genome-wide significance after replication (P=8.8e-8).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Investigate functional significance of noncoding variant.

evidence.quote_or_span: dditional 140 BAV cases from the University of Michigan FCVC biobank were collected. These samples were genotyped using the same GWAS array as the discovery cohort, but were only examined for the three variants described here.
In the Montreal Heart Institute (MHI) biobank, 305 BAV cases and 2,746 controls were collected and genotyped on the Illumina Core Exome array
In the Partners HealthCare cohort, 452 Caucasian BAV cases were identified from the electronic medical records (EMRs) of Partners HealthCare
In the University of Texas Health Science Centre cohort, 765 patients with sporadic TAAs or aortic dissections were collected and genotyped.
Ser377Gly (rs3729856) in GATA4, which encodes a cardiac-specific transcription factor that is 151 kilobases(kb) away from the first variant ( $P_{after-replication}=8.8\times10^{-8}$ ).

We first identified potentially functional variants using RegulomeDB and HaploReg in the region near rs6601627 or variants in high LD ( $r^{2}>0.6$ ; ranging from rs112197605 to rs117851931; hg19:chr8:11774952–11838697) $^{41,42}$ .

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Noncoding variant rs6601627 associated with BAV.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which variant in the region is functional?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A variant in high LD with rs6601627 may be functional.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Identify potentially functional variants using RegulomeDB and HaploReg.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Identified rs118065347 as a likely functional variant co-localizing with multiple transcription factor binding sites and in a known enhancer active in fetal heart.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Investigate chromatin interactions of the region.

evidence.quote_or_span: we identified rs118065347 as a variant likely to be functional because it co-localizes with binding regions for multiple transcription factors (including KAP1, CCNT2, CJUN, C-MYC, GATA2, HDAC2, HMGN3, JUND, MAX, SP1, TAL1, YY1 and ZBTB7A) and is in a known enhancer active in fetal heart, left and right ventricle, right atrium, as well as other tissues

We first identified potentially functional variants using RegulomeDB and HaploReg in the region near rs6601627 or variants in high LD ( $r^{2}>0.6$ ; ranging from rs112197605 to rs117851931; hg19:chr8:11774952–11838697) $^{41,42}$ .

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does this region interact with GATA4?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The region containing rs118065347 loops to GATA4.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Chromatin interaction loops show that the region containing rs118065347 loops to an enhancer near GATA4, suggesting potential regulatory interaction.

evidence.quote_or_span: The variant falls inside a ChIA-PET loop connecting to a region also annotated as an enhancer 3' of GATA4 and C8orf49 and 5' of NEIL2.
These data indicate that this distal region is brought in close proximity to GATA4 and disruption of this region may have direct impact on GATA4 expression.

we identified rs118065347 as a variant likely to be functional because it co-localizes with binding regions for multiple transcription factors (including KAP1, CCNT2, CJUN, C-MYC, GATA2, HDAC2, HMGN3, JUND, MAX, SP1, TAL1, YY1 and ZBTB7A) and is in a known enhancer active in fetal heart, left and right ventricle, right atrium, as well as other tissues

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Chromatin looping evidence suggests regulatory interaction with GATA4.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How does the noncoding variant affect BAV?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Noncoding variant disrupts a regulatory element affecting GATA4 expression during valve development, possibly impairing EndoMT.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Propose mechanism and test GATA4 role in EndoMT.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test GATA4 role in EndoMT using CRISPR-Cas9.

evidence.quote_or_span: We propose that this noncoding variant, or a variant tagged by it, influences GATA4 expression in a manner not detectable by GTE—either exerting an influence on gene expression levels only in the developing fetal heart or with a relatively modest effect that was not detectable in the current GTEx sample size.

chromatin conformation experiments suggest that the region near the associated variants appears to loop and physically interact with regions intronic to GATA4. This hypothesis could be tested in future functional experiments to investigate whether the noncoding BAV-associated variants identified here affect expression of GATA4 at a critical time in heart development. This could possibly disrupt EndoMT, a process important for normal trileaflet aortic valve formation.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis that GATA4 disruption impairs EndoMT.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: CRISPR-Cas9 disruption of GATA4 in iPSC-derived endothelial cells will impair EndoMT.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform CRISPR-Cas9 knockout of GATA4 in iPSC-derived endothelial cells and assess EndoMT.
evolution_trajectory.observation: GATA4 sgRNA group showed significantly fewer mesenchymal cells migrating out after 3 days than control cells.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Acknowledge limitation that mechanism of noncoding variant not confirmed.

evidence.quote_or_span: The GATA4 sgRNA group showed significantly fewer mesenchymal cells migrating out after 3 days than control cells
We demonstrated that deficiency of GATA4 impaired the EndoMTs, a critical step in valve formation
Numbers of mesenchymal cells from control and GATA4 sgRNA in collagen gel assay. The data were normalized to control.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: GATA4 disruption impairs EndoMT, but noncoding variant mechanism not directly tested.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Mechanism of action of noncoding variant on aortic valve development remains unconfirmed.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Future functional experiments are needed to confirm the mechanism.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Cannot yet confirm the mechanism of action of the noncoding variant(s) on chromosome 8 on aortic valve development.

evidence.quote_or_span: Although we cannot yet confirm the mechanism of action of the noncoding variant(s) on chromosome 8 on aortic valve development

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Replication included BioMe cohort with non-European samples.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Generalizability to non-European populations uncertain due to few non-European cases.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Results may not generalize to non-European populations.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Present results excluding non-European samples due to few cases.
evolution_trajectory.observation: BioMe replication had few non-European cases, so results were presented excluding them; generalizability to non-European populations uncertain.

evidence.quote_or_span: We present results in this study excluding the non-European samples since there were few cases.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Missense variant rs3729856 associated with BAV.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Assess predicted impact of rs3729856 using PolyPhen-2, SIFT, and CADD.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Missense variant rs3729856 predicted benign/tolerated by PolyPhen-2 and SIFT, CADD score 9.418 (top 11% deleterious); functional significance unclear.

evidence.quote_or_span: 20) and SIFT $^{21}$ , and it has a combined annotation dependent depletion (CADD) score 9.418 (in top 11% of deleterious variants in the human genome) $^{22}$ .
Ser377Gly in GATA4 (rs3729856) that was also associated with BAV ( $P_{discovery} = 3.

validation_methods: Functional validation via CRISPR-Cas9 knockout in iPSC-derived endothelial cells
success_verification.final_conclusion: Identified a low-frequency noncoding variant rs6601627 and a common missense variant rs3729856 in GATA4 associated with BAV. Functional experiments show GATA4 deficiency impairs EndoMT, but the mechanism of the noncoding variant remains unconfirmed.
limitations: Mechanism of noncoding variant not confirmed
BioMe replication had few non-European cases, limiting generalizability
Missense variant rs3729856 predicted benign/tolerated by PolyPhen-2 and SIFT, CADD score 9.418 (top 11% deleterious); functional significance unclear

risk_notes: Some evidence quotes are truncated in the evidence bank; full quotes may provide additional context.
The paper is a genetic association study with functional follow-up, not a protein engineering study, but the trajectory is evidence-grounded.
